name: Western Union Money Order Only
description_short: Western Union operates a global money transfer and payment services network with 500,000+ agent locations offering money orders, cash pickup, bill payment, and wire transfers.
description_long: Western Union is a multinational financial services company that has operated money transfer and payment services for over 150 years. The company operates through an extensive agent network including major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Circle K, and Kroger, providing financial services access in communities worldwide. Their core offerings include international and domestic money transfers, cash pickup services, money order issuance, bill payment processing, and mobile top-up services. The company has modernized to offer both in-store and online transaction initiation, with their WU.com platform allowing customers to start transfers 24/7 online and complete them in-store based on agent hours. What distinguishes Western Union is the scale and accessibility of their agent network—with locations in grocery stores, convenience stores, and major retailers rather than standalone offices—making services available during extended retail hours. They also offer specialized services like inmate payment processing, prepaid card options, and Western Union Rewards loyalty programs. However, Western Union is primarily a money transfer and payment services provider rather than a comprehensive financial institution. Their business model relies on agent partnerships, which means service availability and hours depend entirely on individual retailer locations. For consumers seeking money orders specifically, Western Union offers this service at select locations, though the website content indicates some locations offer "Money Order Services only," suggesting variable service availability across the network.
address: 1165 E Anaheim St, Long Beach, CA 90813

company_info.headquarters: 1165 E Anaheim St, Long Beach, CA 90813

pros: Extensive network of 500,000+ agent locations globally, including major retailers with extended hours
24/7 online transaction initiation through WU.com platform with in-store completion option
Multiple service options: money orders, wire transfers, bill payment, mobile top-up, and inmate payment
Locations at convenient retail partners (Walmart, Safeway, Circle K) often open until 8-11:59 PM
Western Union Rewards loyalty program for frequent users
Support for multiple languages including English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, and Vietnamese
Real-time transfer tracking capability for sent and received funds
cons: Service availability varies significantly by location—some agents offer money orders only, others offer full service menu
Operating hours depend on retail partner hours, not dedicated financial services hours
Website does not display pricing or fee information for money orders or other services
No indication of customer service responsiveness or dispute resolution processes on location finder
Geolocation feature requires browser permissions and currently shows limitations in location accuracy
best_for: International money senders and recipients needing accessible cash pickup locations
Unbanked or underbanked consumers using retail-based financial service access points
Customers needing bill payment or mobile top-up services during extended retail hours
Individuals specifically seeking money order services at convenient neighborhood retailers
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who need accessible, geographically convenient access to money transfer, cash pickup, and money order services through a nationwide retail network rather than traditional banking channels. The main caveat is that service offerings vary significantly by location—consumers must verify that their specific agent location provides the exact service needed (particularly money orders), as some locations offer limited menus, and availability depends entirely on retail partner hours.
services: Money order issuance (available at select locations)
